title: AI-Accelerated Drug Discovery for Neglected Tropical Diseases in Sub-Saharan Africa
authors: Okafor C, Mensah K, Diallo A, Magami OS Consortium
excerpt: A comprehensive study demonstrating how AI-driven screening identified three promising drug candidates for NTDs prevalent in Sub-Saharan Africa.
abstract:
We present a framework for AI-accelerated drug discovery targeting neglected tropical diseases (NTDs) prevalent in Sub-Saharan Africa. Using a combination of deep learning models for molecular property prediction and virtual screening, we identified seven novel compounds with activity against Schistosoma mansoni and Onchocerca volvulus. Three candidates showed favourable pharmacokinetic profiles and low predicted toxicity in preliminary in silico assays.

Our approach demonstrates that AI-driven

title: A Scalable Training Framework for AI in Biomedical Research: Lessons from the Magami OS Initiative
authors: Diallo A, Okafor C, Mensah K
excerpt: A retrospective analysis of our training framework for biomedical researchers across Africa, covering curriculum design, delivery methods, and measured outcomes.
abstract: We present a retrospective analysis of the Magami OS training framework, which has reached over 200 biomedical researchers across 12 African countries. The curriculum covers foundational machine learning, molecular modelling, high-performance computing, and ethical AI practices. We report quantitative outcomes including pre- and post-training assessment scores, successfully completed collaborative projects, and participant-reported impact on their research workflows. title: Magami OS: An Open-Source Platform for AI-Assisted Drug Discovery in Africa
authors: Mensah K, Okafor C, Diallo A, et al.
excerpt: Introducing the Magami OS platform, an open-source toolkit designed specifically for African researchers conducting AI-assisted drug discovery.
abstract:
The Magami OS platform is an integrated open-source software ecosystem designed to lower the barrier to AI-assisted drug discovery for researchers across Africa. The platform comprises three core modules: (i) a molecular library management system pre-loaded with compound databases relevant to African diseases, (ii) a suite of machine learning models for property prediction and lead optimization, and (iii) a collaborative workspace for distributed research teams.
